name: Bearnaise Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Clarify the butter by heating it slowly over low heat. When it stops foaming, it's clarified, and you can pour off the golden liquid fat on top; the solids will remain at the bottom of the pan. Keep warm.
2. In a small saucepan, heat the red wine vinegar, white wine vinegar, shallots, tarragon, salt, and pepper over low heat until all the liquid has reduced by half.
3. In a stainless steel bowl set over a double boiler with simmering water, beat the egg yolks with 1 tablespoon of water until pale and thickened, creating a ribbon-like consistency.
4. Gradually stir in the clarified butter into the egg yolk mixture, whisking continuously to incorporate smoothly, similar to making mayonnaise.
5. Mix in the reduction from step 2 and adjust seasonings with additional salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed. Keep the sauce warm for up to three hours before serving.
